overview: Add namespace-name filtering to `viewnode ns list` through a local `--filter` / `-f` flag using case-insensitive substring matching, and replace generic filtered empty-state output with clear success-path no-match messages for namespace listing and the existing root node-filter flow.

userStories.title: Wire namespace filter flags on ns list
userStories.description: Add a local namespace filter flag to `viewnode ns list` so users can request filtered namespace output with `--filter` or `-f` without changing the existing root node-filter contract.
acceptanceCriteria: `viewnode ns list --filter <value>` is accepted by the `ns list` command.
`viewnode ns list -f <value>` is accepted by the `ns list` command.
The existing root `--node-filter` flag name and meaning remain unchanged.
userStories.notes: Keep flag wiring local to `cmd/ns/list.go` so shorthand `-f` is scoped to namespace listing only.

userStories.title: Implement case-insensitive namespace filtering
userStories.description: Filter namespace results by case-insensitive substring matching after the existing namespace list call while preserving alphabetical ordering and active namespace marker behavior.
acceptanceCriteria: Running `viewnode ns list --filter kube` returns only namespaces whose names contain `kube`, regardless of letter case.
Filtered namespace output remains alphabetically ordered.
Active namespace marking continues to treat an empty active kubeconfig namespace as `default`.
userStories.notes: Reuse the current namespace entry preparation path and avoid adding extra Kubernetes API requests.

userStories.title: Normalize empty namespace filter behavior
userStories.description: Treat an empty namespace filter value as equivalent to omitting the filter so blank input does not produce surprising results or command errors.
acceptanceCriteria: An empty namespace filter value produces the same output as running `viewnode ns list` without a filter.
Empty filter handling does not turn `viewnode ns list` into an error state.
Unfiltered namespace list behavior remains unchanged.
userStories.notes: Implement this normalization in the existing `cmd/ns/list.go` flow alongside filter matching.

userStories.title: Add clear filtered no-match messages
userStories.description: Replace generic filtered empty-state text with clear messages that explain when namespace or node filters matched no resources while keeping command execution successful.
acceptanceCriteria: When `viewnode ns list` finds no namespaces matching the filter, it prints a clear no-match message and exits successfully.
When the root node-filter flow finds no nodes matching the filter, it prints a clear no-match message instead of `no nodes to display...`.
Filtered no-match outcomes remain distinguishable from namespace discovery or cluster access failures.
userStories.notes: Use minimal additional context from `cmd/root.go` into `srv/view.go` and avoid restructuring the existing printout pipeline.

userStories.title: Add regression coverage for filter behavior
userStories.description: Add automated tests that lock in namespace filter flag behavior, case-insensitive matching, empty-filter fallback, and filter-aware no-match messaging using repository-approved Cobra test patterns.
acceptanceCriteria: Automated tests cover `--filter` and `-f` equivalence for `viewnode ns list`.
Automated tests cover case-insensitive namespace matching and empty-filter fallback.
Automated tests cover filtered no-match messaging for namespace and node flows.
Cobra tests that depend on inherited flags execute through a small root command tree.

userStories.title: Update README and run repository validation
userStories.description: Document the new namespace filtering behavior in the manually maintained README and finish the feature with repository-level validation.
acceptanceCriteria: `README.md` documents `viewnode ns list --filter` and `viewnode ns list -f` with at least one filtered namespace example.
`README.md` reflects the clearer filtered no-match behavior where user-facing examples are relevant.
Running `make test` succeeds after the feature changes are complete.
userStories.notes: Keep documentation updates in the same change as the CLI behavior update, per repository guidance.
